consumer_hint: Node v159 read-only capture release evidence review

stable_fields.meaning: Version of the INFOJSON response shape.
stable_fields.control_plane_use: Reject or quarantine unsupported response shapes before release review.

stable_fields.meaning: The command only reports runtime state.
stable_fields.control_plane_use: Must be true before treating the response as read-only capture evidence.

stable_fields.meaning: The response does not grant permission to execute write commands.
stable_fields.control_plane_use: Must remain false while production operations are disabled.

stable_fields.meaning: mini-kv is not the order-system source of truth.
stable_fields.control_plane_use: Must remain false when Node combines Java and mini-kv evidence.

stable_fields.meaning: Labels the response as runtime identity evidence.
stable_fields.control_plane_use: Use runtime_identity for version, protocol, WAL, metrics, and request-limit interpretation.

stable_fields.meaning: mini-kv build version injected by CMake.
stable_fields.control_plane_use: Compare with the expected mini-kv tag used by the release evidence review.

stable_fields.meaning: Protocols enabled for the current command processor.
stable_fields.control_plane_use: Expect inline for local fixtures and inline plus resp for the TCP server.

stable_fields.meaning: Configured TCP request byte limit, or zero for inline-only processors.
stable_fields.control_plane_use: Treat as runtime configuration evidence, not business data.

stable_fields.meaning: Current live key count after normal expiration pruning.
stable_fields.control_plane_use: Use as runtime storage signal only; never treat as order count.

stable_fields.meaning: Whether this processor has a WAL attached.
stable_fields.control_plane_use: Use to explain persistence mode for the observed mini-kv process.

stable_fields.meaning: Whether periodic metrics output is enabled for the current runtime.
stable_fields.control_plane_use: Use to explain why metrics stream evidence may or may not exist.

stable_fields.meaning: The INFOJSON command did not execute any write command.
stable_fields.control_plane_use: Must be false for read-only capture evidence.

dynamic_fields.comparison_rule: Check type and presence; do not compare exact fixture value in live capture.

stable_fields.meaning: Version of the STATSJSON response shape.
stable_fields.control_plane_use: Reject or quarantine unsupported metrics response shapes before release review.

stable_fields.meaning: The command reports counters without mutating user data.
stable_fields.control_plane_use: Must be true before treating the response as read-only capture evidence.

stable_fields.meaning: The response does not grant permission to execute write commands.
stable_fields.control_plane_use: Must remain false while production operations are disabled.

stable_fields.meaning: Metrics do not make mini-kv an order-authoritative store.
stable_fields.control_plane_use: Must remain false in cross-project release evidence.

stable_fields.meaning: Labels the response as runtime metrics evidence.
stable_fields.control_plane_use: Use runtime_metrics for command, latency, WAL, and connection-counter interpretation.

stable_fields.meaning: Current live key count as a runtime metric.
stable_fields.control_plane_use: Use as observed KV state only; not as business order state.

stable_fields.meaning: Fast boolean for WAL presence.
stable_fields.control_plane_use: Pair with the detailed wal object or null value.

stable_fields.meaning: WAL maintenance snapshot when WAL is enabled, otherwise null.
stable_fields.control_plane_use: Use for persistence diagnostics; absence is valid when mini-kv is memory-only.

stable_fields.meaning: Total commands observed before this STATSJSON command is accounted.
stable_fields.control_plane_use: Use as runtime counter evidence; it can grow between captures.

stable_fields.meaning: Whether TCP connection counters were supplied by the server.
stable_fields.control_plane_use: Expect false for inline fixtures and true for TCP server captures.

stable_fields.meaning: The STATSJSON command did not execute any write command.
stable_fields.control_plane_use: Must be false for read-only capture evidence.

dynamic_fields.comparison_rule: Check numeric type and monotonic plausibility; do not compare exact fixture value in live capture.
Check numeric type; exact value is process and machine dependent.
Check numeric type; exact value is process and machine dependent.

dynamic_fields.reason: Per-command latency counters depend on the observed command sequence.
dynamic_fields.comparison_rule: Check shape and command buckets relevant to the smoke run.

release_semantics: This guide explains read-only capture evidence only.
Skipped or mixed capture evidence is not a production pass.
No mini-kv response in this package authorizes writes or production operations.

notes: INFOJSON and STATSJSON are the primary real-read capture commands for mini-kv.
CHECKJSON remains useful for proving a target command contract without executing it.
HEALTH is liveness evidence and must not be promoted to order-authoritative evidence.
The field guide is explanatory evidence for Node v159 release review, not a new server endpoint.
